records.ety: [Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to make two parties join against a third: cf. F. syncrtisme.]

defs: Attempted union of principles or parties irreconcilably at variance with each other.
quotes: He is plotting a carnal syncretism, and attempting the reconcilement of Christ and Belial.
Syncretism is opposed to eclecticism in philosophy.

defs: The union or fusion into one of two or more originally different inflectional forms, as of two cases.
